INTRODUCTION TO STATS
If there is one thing that Grant Cardone obsesses about in business, it is statistics.
In a world full of noise and shiny objects, Grant lets the numbers do the talking
every time.
Managing by statistics allows you to…
Focus on what matters most and avoid wasting your precious time. Using the
methods enclosed, Grant is able to run a multitude of profitable companies
simultaneously, all while enjoying his life.
Operate More Effectively
As Grant likes to say, “The bottom line is the bottom line.” Grant’s methods
have taken his educational company over 9-figures and his real estate
company, Cardone Capital, into the multi-billions.
Produce Better Results
Revenue growth in just one of Grant’s many merchant accounts
If you don’t feel like you are as on top of statistics as you should be, you are not
alone. Nearly every entrepreneur and manager understands the importance of
stats, yet its reported that only 14% track them regularly.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

5
What you pay attention to will grow. What you neglect will die.
If you aren’t tracking statistics and reviewing them constantly,
you will never become a world-class performer. Period.
What that means for you is that you have a big opportunity to outperform your
competition. While it won’t be easy, the 10X Stats System will give you the edge.
Follow the strategies and steps inside the program and watch your business 10X!
Let’s do this!

-- 5 of 48 --

MEASURING STATISTICS
Statistics come in many forms. Every business will track different statistics and utilize
them in different ways. Let’s identify what stats matter most for your company.
Before getting into the minutia, you must first understand Grant Cardone’s biggest
secret to success. Of everything we will cover, this simple lesson is by far the most
important…
A stat is simply a number. A target is a specific goal that drives you, and everyone
around you, to take massive action and achieve 10X levels of success.
© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
6
BEING TARGET DRIVEN
TARGETS AND KPIS
Having clear and compelling targets
is the key to driving explosive growth.
Your targets must be significant
If a target doesn’t excite you, and even make you a little nervous, it is not big enough.
Your targets must be measurable
Focus on targets that are easy to track, like sales, rather than ones that
involve calculations.
Your targets must be urgent
To be a target, there is always an end date. Ideally it is a significant event to
run toward.
Your targets must be actionable
Select only a few critical targets that you can directly influence the outcome
with effort.
Your targets must be communicated
Post your company targets everywhere and discuss them constantly so they
are top of mind.
Your targets must be non-negotiable
Once established, targets are in stone. Your target is your minimum and you
hit it or you don’t.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 48 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
7
Remember this: you should never adjust your targets, you
should only increase your efforts to reach your targets.
Give it all you’ve got to the last minute, every time.
Not only should every department in your company track
stats, every department should make sales as well. We built
a culture where everyone sells and everyone gets paid.
Statistics come in many forms. Every business will track different statistics and utilize
them in different ways. Let’s identify what stats matter most for your company.
If you look at Grant’s targets and think they are a little crazy, it is because they are.
Setting massive targets and taking massive action is at the core of the 10X Rule. While
your targets may not be nearly as large, they should stretch you in much the same way
that Grant’s did for his organizations.
While targets are what drive a 10X operation, it is important to understand that there
are many statistics that make up a target.
For example, if your target is to do $1,000,000 in sales for the year you are going to need
to spend money on ads, generate leads, make phone sales, support customers, etc, etc.
Each of those activities will have its own set of statistics.
10X Example of Grant’s Past Targets:
Sell out all 35,000 GrowthCon tickets before event
Generate 1,000 new coaching leads this month
Hit 2,000,000 YouTube subscribers by end of year
Do over $100,000,000 in annual company revenue
Get $5 Billion in real estate under management
ESTABLISHING YOUR KPIS

Here are some basic steps to get you going…
Below are a few sample stats to get you started. Keep in mind that the names and
details of a particular stat may vary, as well as the period of time for which a statistic
is tracked.
As you will see, there are a lot of moving pieces in statistical tracking and reporting.
Always put the first things first. Do not let all the details get in the way of focusing
on what matters most. You could stop here and still 10X your business.
STEP #1 	ESTABLISH YOUR KEY TARGETS
To get things started you should make a master list of every statistic involved in
your business. We’ve started a list below to help with your brainstorming, but
remember that every organization will be different. Think of all the moving pieces.
STEP #2 	LIST OUT EVERY COMPANY STATISTICS
Once you’ve created your stats list, you’ll want to identify your Key Performance
Indicators (KPIs). These are the statistics that are most essential for your business.
In the 10X office we often refer to these as the “needle movers”.
STEP #3 	IDENTIFY YOUR MOST IMPORTANT KPIS
SALES STATISTICS
� Total revenue amount
� Number of new buyers
� Sales conversion rate
� Average cart value
� Monthly recurring revenue
LEAD STATISTICS
� New email opt-ins
� Qualified sales leads
� Total email subscribers
� Applications submitted
� Text message opt-ins
TRAFFIC STATISTICS
� Total website visits
� Visitors from organic search
� Percent returning visitors
� Average time on site
� Bounce rate
ADVERTISING STATISTICS
� Total advertising cost
� Cost per click
� Cost per acquisition
� Return on ad spend
� Net profitability

Keep in mind that while you will ultimately track
all aspects of your business, it won’t happen
overnight. We’ve been working on our tracking
and reporting systems for years, and we’re still
making improvements. Remember your
priorities…
The reason so few companies succeed with stats is because they get bogged down

10
Too many entrepreneurs focus on all the wrong numbers.
Cash flow is king. Know your revenues and expenses like the
back of your hand: yearly, monthly, weekly and even daily.
Once you’ve identified what you want to track, you need to get clarity on where this
information will come from. For your business, this will likely come from a combination
of systems. Some of these systems you may already have implemented, while others
may need to be put in place.
Here are some examples of where your data will come from…
While we will discuss some of these in great detail, every business will be different.
Here are a few things to keep in mind when identifying your primary data sources...
Some data sources may have overlap. For example, Google Analytics might report some
of the same stats as Clickfunnels. What matters most is that you establish what your
“source of truth” will be, and you use it consistently.
CLARIFYING DATA SOURCES
Bookkeeping or financial software (such as Quickbooks)
Sales funnels and websites (such as Wordpress or Clickfunnels)
CRM and/or email marketer (such as Hubspot or Mailchimp)
Credit card merchant accounts (such as Stripe or Paypal)
Advertising accounts (such as Facebook Ads or Google Ads)
Social media accounts (such as YouTube or Instagram)
Web analytics software (such as Google Analytics)
Shipping and inventory software (such as Shipstation)
Tickets and Customer Support (such as Zendesk)
Example of basic stats provided inside Grant’s YouTube account
Sometimes there will be multiple sources of information.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 48 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
11
For example, the statistics in Clickfunnels aren’t perfect (no statistics really are for that
matter). We use Clickfunnels stats for most of our day-to-day discussion, but when it
matters for a major decision, we’ll pull data from our merchant account.
Some data sources are more accurate than others.
As we’ll discuss later, some systems will allow you to integrate more easily with stats
dashboards. So the data source you use today for manually tracking stats, may change
later when you automate your stats reporting.
Some data sources are easier to work with than others.
While technology is great, there is nothing wrong with doing
things the old fashioned way. I made millions of dollars
tracking sales on a white board before I learned any of this.
Stats dashboard on Grant’s primary Clickfunnels account
While nearly every stat is able to eventually be automated, even statistics that happen
offline, at first some things will be tracked manually. Don’t overlook this type of data
because oftentimes this sort of manual activity is the best place to start.
Some stats will have no data source and will be tracked manually.

12
At this point we’ve discussed which statistics you should track and where your data will
come from. We will now explore how you will access your statistics in the form of reports.
While reporting will vary widely depending on many variables, there are certain
universal factors involved that you will need to understand.
Without having a specific time period, there is no way to clearly measure a result. When
using dashboards, either those provided by various systems or ones you build yourself,
you are able to define specific dates ranges. If done manually, you’ll stick to pre-defined
time windows such as daily, weekly, monthly, or yearly.
As you may have already realized, stats come in many forms. Some statistics are direct
results (raw data) while others are derived mathematically (calculated data). While large
corporations utilize many complex computations, most small business formulas are very
simple. Here are a few examples…
GENERAL REPORTING PRINCIPLES
STATISTICAL REPORTING
Reporting Window
Date Range vs. Standard Periods
Raw Data vs. Calculated Data
Example of date range selector inside of Facebook Ads
Data Type

-- 12 of 48 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
13
Numeric Stats vs. Visual Graphics
Display Type
Example Sales Floor Statistics
Raw Data Collected:
Number of Leads
Number of Sales Made
Total Sales Revenue
KPIs from Calculated Data:
Earnings Per Lead
Average Sale Amount
Closing Percentage
Formulas Used to Calculate KPIs:
Total Sales Revenue ➗ Number of Leads = Earnings Per Lead
Total Sales Revenue ➗ Number of Sales Made = Average Sale Amount
Number of Sales Made ➗ Number of Leads = Closing Percentage
Again, the actual names of a statistic may vary.
The name is less important than the data!
Having the numbers is great, but the reality is that most people are visual. In order to
get your reports in a place where you can quickly and easily interpret the data, it is very
helpful to use charts and graphs. As you can see below, there are many ways to display
information visually, each having an ideal use.

Reports provided in various software are great for day-to-day
because that is where the work is done. To get the bigger picture,
you’ll want KPIs combined in a spreadsheet or dashboard.
Keep in mind there is no exact protocol for how you should manage your stats. Your
situation will differ based on business type, business size, and the resources you have
available. You will likely utilize a combination of these methods. That said,
understanding this general progression will help you understand where you should
start and where you should be heading.
This is where you will generally start because you most likely have access to most of
these stats already. The majority of the data sources discussed earlier will already have
their own statistical dashboard. This includes CRMs, email marketers, funnel builders,
bookkeeping, etc.
Some systems will have default stat reporting that works “out-of-the-box”. Others will
allow you to create custom reports, such as the Hubspot report shown above.
Regardless, the most important thing is that you know what reports to run and that you
review them regularly.
Hubspot is an advanced CRM that allows for a wide range of reporting
To provide specific examples, these are some of the systems we use for reporting…

Clickfunnels
Hubspot

Instagram

For most businesses, the biggest area of mystery is web analytics. To
understand your traffic and online conversions we recommend using Google
Analytics. This will require some setup, but the good news is we will walk you
through it in the next chapter!
If you are going to keep stats in a spreadsheet, it is highly recommended
that you use Google Sheets. It gives you all the capability of Excel, but makes
online collaboration easy. Be sure to watch the Google Sheets tutorial video
for some quick tips!
While you can view most stats within your various software, it requires logging into
many different systems and does not provide a comprehensive view of all your KPIs.
For this reason, many businesses utilize spreadsheets to summarize their most
important statistics.
Daily statistics for one of Grant’s training webinars
LEVEL 2 REPORTING METHOD
Manually Compiled in Spreadsheets
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 48 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
17
While we generally use spreadsheets for compiling data manually, it is
possible to automate data feeds. To do this, use a program called Zapier
which integrates nearly any type of online program into Google Sheets.
That said, if you are going to set up integrations, you may want to just skip
to level 3 and use Data Studio!
While there are many dashboard platforms on the market, we recommend
Google Data Studio. Not only is it free, it is extremely powerful and relatively
easy to use. Data Studio is the ultimate weapon for a CEO or CMO!
In the example above, statistics were pulled from multiple places including the webinar,
sales funnel and email systems. While there is work involved in consolidating this type of
data, having all the data in one place allows you to make better decisions.
When you’ve outgrown spreadsheets, and you have the resources, it is time to automate
your reporting. Automated dashboards allow you to pull in data from any number of
channels and design reports in any way you want.
LEVEL 3 REPORTING METHOD
Real-Time Automated Dashboards
Example of stats and chart for Grant’s newest website in Data Studio

don’t live in a perfect world. Automate the things that matter
most, and don’t let technology get in the way of progress.
Remember, you can use a combination of these methods to achieve your goal. Rome
wasn’t built in a day. We will discuss strategies for implementation and management
more in a later chapter.
As you’ve already learned, we use Google products to manage our stats. We recommend
you do too and here’s why…

They provide a complete integrated solution
They are widely used, making hiring easy
They work seamlessly with Google Ads

I highly recommend you keep yourself organized. Don’t
sign up for a bunch of extra accounts, and only give
people access as is needed. This stuff can get messy fast.
We will focus on Tag Manager, Analytics, and Data Studio as they are the primary tools
used to collect and manage statistics. It is important to understand how these tools
work together, as well as the ideal order to set them up…
Google Analytics
Google Data Studio
Google Tag Manager

Here are the different tools that Google provides…
TAG MANAGER	1
Tag Manager is installed on your website, allowing
you to easily add Analytics and other marketing tools.
ANALYTICS	2
Once you add Tag Manager to your site, you will add
Analytics to Tag Manager, allowing you to collect web data.
DATA STUDIO	3
Once you are receiving data in Analytics, you can use
that data to create customized reports in Data Studio.

Let’s start by answering some important questions…
In order to capture any sort of data from your websites, including conversion events and
advertising data, you must install small pieces of code. Sometimes this code is referred
to as a “snippet” (for Google Analytics or Ads for example) and sometimes it is referred to
as a “pixel” (for Facebook Ads for example). A “tag” is the general term that is used to
describe the installation of any of these codes.
Since most marketers don’t like to touch code (maybe you’re one of them!), Google
created Tag Manager to simplify the process. Instead of trying to edit code every time a
change is needed, or needing to bother a programmer to do it for you, you simply install
Google Tag Manager once and then manage all your tags from the Tag Manager
interface.
TAG MANAGER BASICS
GOOGLE TAG MANAGER
What the heck is a tag and why do I need them?
What does Google Tag Manager do?
Don’t worry, the installation code is not as bad as it looks
Example of tags set up in Tag Manager with no coding
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 48 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
21
Like all Google marketing products, you’ll need to have a Google account (an email
address connected to Google). To get started with Tag Manager go to 10xresource.com/tm
and give your account a name, most likely your company name.
During signup you’ll be asked to create what is called a Container. Containers are where
you manage your tags for a particular website or app. Generally speaking, it is
recommended that you set up a new container for each website you manage. We name
our containers as our website domains.
How do I create an account and what is a Container?
While installation will vary slightly depending on what type of website you have (for
example, Wordpress or Clickfunnels) the process is generally the same. You’ll start by
going to the Admin section in Tag Manager and clicking on the install option to see
the code…
On the installation page, you’ll see a link to Google’s instructions. Keep in mind
that all website or funnel platforms can be a little bit different. You may want to do
a quick Google search for the specific system you use. For example, “install tag
manager on Wordpress”. If you use Clickfunnels go here… 10xresource.com/s21
You then have 2 options…
How do I install Tag Manager on my sites and/or funnels?
If you are tracking a website always choose Web
OPTION 1 - Install the Code Yourself
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 48 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
22
If the thought of copy and pasting code into a box is too daunting, you can hire
someone. If you have a technical person on your team, simply copy and paste the
instructions and send it to them. If not, an inexpensive option is called Fiverr. They
have several service providers who specialize in this… 10xresource.com/s22
OPTION 2 - Outsource the Installation
Once you’ve got Tag Manager installed on your websites and funnels, it is time to
configure your tags. There are 3 basic components involved in the setup…
While we’ve provided videos to teach you how to set up your tags, here is a little more
context of how this works…
While you can add virtually any type of code onto your sites or pages using tag
manager, you’ll primarily install tags for two reasons…
HOW TAGS WORK
→ 	What you want it to achieve
→ 	What causes the tag to fire
→ 	Optional values attached to tags
Tags
To connect other platforms to your website
Examples: Analytics, Google Ads, Facebook Pixel,
Pinterest Tag
Setup Note: This type of tag is usually added to
all pages of your website
A
To track specific actions (referred to as “events”)
Examples: Analytics Events, Facebook Ad Events
Setup Note: This type of tag is usually added to
individual pages
B
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 48 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
23
In order for you to set up a tag, you will need to have a trigger to make the tag fire. The
most commonly used trigger is a web page. So for example, if you are triggering a
purchase tag, you would install it on the thank you page that comes after the purchase.
Triggers
Variables are used for both triggers and tags. For triggers, a variable defines when a
tag should fire. For example, when the URL contains example.com/thanks. For tags, a
variable can capture values. For example, the amount of a sale or the name of a
particular promotion.
Variables
Used most commonly
Used for button clicks (advanced)

Google Analytics is the industry standard for tracking website and app statistics.
Analytics provides a wide range of reports and is highly customizable. It also can send
data into Google Data Studio, allowing you to create even more robust reporting.
In regards to set up, Google Analytics is organizationally structured with two levels…
Account - Accounts are the highest level. You will likely only want one Account per
company.
Properties - Properties are where you view statistics, create reports and manage all
primary functions. We recommend that you create a new Property for each website or
app you manage.
ANALYTICS BASICS
GOOGLE ANALYTICS
Analytics Account 	Properties
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Once you’ve created your Analytics account and property, the first order of
duty is to connect it to your website via Tag Manager. We’ve provided details
on this in the Tag Manager section as well as on the video tutorial. You can
also learn more here: 10xresource.com/s15
Google Tag Manager
Sample report cards displayed on Analytics home page
Tools like Google Analytics are visual. Reading about it is good,
watching videos is better, but nothing beats the hands-on
approach. Just get in there and start playing with it!
NOTE: Google releases a new version of Analytics from time to time, and the latest
version is GA4. It replaced the version referred to as “Universal Analytics”, and includes
several significant changes. This manual references GA4.
The good news is Analytics 4 is extremely easy to navigate…
NAVIGATION AND FUNCTIONALITY

You can review a wide variety of reports in Analytics out of the box. The primary report
categories are…

Reports that provide insight about users experience on your site.	ENGAGEMENT
Reports that provide details on purchase transactions.	MONETIZATION
Reports that show how long users stay active on your site.	MONETIZATION

As you may have noticed, reports in Analytics are displayed in boxes. These boxes are
called cards. Here are some of the card types you can add…
Report cards can be dragged and dropped to display in any order.
Reports can be edited based on many factors. Keep in mind that some of the most
valuable reports, specifically Conversions, will require setup. Also, you may choose not to
use reports inside of Analytics. Analytics can be easily connected to Data Studio, or you
can report the most important KPIs regularly in Google Sheets.

Prepare to play the long game with ads, otherwise it is painful.
It takes time. We now have days where we lose money on our
ads, but we make hundreds of thousands of dollars.
The Explore section is full of reports that can unlock a wealth of knowledge. For
example, the Path Exploration report will give you a deeper understanding of your
customers journey throughout your funnel.
Keep in mind that most of the reports in Explore are advanced. They may be a little
more difficult to understand than those in the Report section. They may also require
more data to be truly valuable. That said, it is certainly worth exploring Explore!
Some of the visual reports available in the Explore section
As with all Google marketing tools, you can integrate your Analytics with your Google
Ads. This will provide additional reporting capability and improve your ability to track
your visitors and customers.
TIP: While most settings in Analytics require no attention, you will definitely
want to connect your Google Ads account. Go to the Admin section (gear
icon) and follow the simple steps to start feeding Ads data into Analytics,
and vice versa.

30
Audiences is not a feature we use a lot, because there are other strategies to achieve
this, including audience creation in Google Ads.
Configure is where the majority of your setup will occur. While Analytics captures much
information automatically, such as traffic channels and demographics, the most
important information needs to be configured.
Events and Conversions - The biggest value of Analytics is tracking performance data,
such as sales or opt-ins. This is where Events and Conversions come in. While we will
cover setup in the next section, here’s the basics…
Events are any type of activity that users complete while on a website or app.
Conversions are Events that are designated as important, such as leads or sales.
After setting up an Event, make it a Conversion by flipping the switch.
Audiences - To help you understand the behavior and performance of a particular
segment of people, Analytics gives you the ability to create Audiences. You can create
Audiences for users that perform a particular event, or based on other factors such as
demographics, traffic source or the device they use.
You can refine audiences by using multiple parameters.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

debugger mode to see what information is being sent into Analytics. Note that this is
usually a tool used by developers.

turned into a Conversion…

This training is the greatest ever made for helping you understand
stats and systems on a high level. When you encounter something
we don’t cover, YouTube is your best friend.
FEATURES AND DOWNLOADS
Here are some additional noteworthy features you’ll find when you dig into Analytics…
Data Streams
Data Streams are how Analytics receives data. When you add the
Analytics configuration tag to Tag Manager, you are adding the website
as a data stream. It is worth noting that in GA4, you can have multiple
data sources for one Property. Do this in situations where you have
multiple domains for one offer.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
33
Realtime
A new feature to GA4, you can now see what is happening on your
website as it is happening! This is extremely valuable in situations such
as product launches or when you go live on social media.
We run several funnels to sell 10X Growth Conference
The time of day can affect both traffic and conversions.
Insight
Throughout Analytics you will see Google’s Insights feature. Insights
use AI to make observations and give advice based on trends and
comparative analysis. While they are worth reading, take them with a
grain of salt. As you can see, they aren’t always accurate.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

unique setup needs. To avoid complication and make the most
of Analytics, you can install this plugin (or better yet, have a

One of the greatest challenges for marketers online is understanding where their
traffic, and therefore their leads and sales, are coming from. Analytics captures this
information in a few different ways, each with its own benefits and downfalls…
There are many different dimensions available in Analytics. Ultimately they are data
fields that give you insight about a user. The three dimensions most commonly used in
Analytics are Source, Campaign and Medium, with the Source field being the most
important. Here are some examples of how Analytics stats look for Channels and the
Source dimension…
While the Source value of a visitor will populate with no work needed, it does not
provide clear enough information to be valuable. To remedy this problem, you can
utilize UTM tracking.
ANALYTICS USER TRACKING
Channels 	Dimensions
Little detail and often incomplete
Fully automated and very clean
Very disorganized out of the box
Can be customized and detailed
As you can see, Channel is missing data and Source is very messy.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 35 of 48 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
36
The key to success with UTM, like any of this data stuff, is
to make a plan and stick to it. Otherwise your stats will
become a mess and cause more confusion than good.
Use the long link for ads and the short link for social or email
UTM tracking is a simple protocol that utilizes special links to collect valuable marketing
data. Standardly you can add up to five fields of data (dimensions) to any link. This is
done by adding information on the end of a URL using a UTM builder.
Note that only source and medium are mandatory fields.
Here is an example of what a UTM link looks like when the five dimension fields are
added to the URL grantcardone.com/10x…
While there are recommended uses for each field, as listed above, there is no exact way
to utilize UTM. The most important thing is that you are consistent. Otherwise your data
will not be of any real value.
You can add the following dimensions to a URL…
USING UTM TRACKING
Google UTM Link Builder: 10xresource.com/s18
• Campaign source* (referrer such as Google, Facebook)
• Campaign medium* (type of media such as email, ads)
• Campaign name (such as ad campaign or email sequence)
• Campaign term (specific keyword used for search ads)
• Campaign content (specific details such as image or video)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 36 of 48 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
37
Knowing where a user comes from is the first step, but the story does not end there.
After all, a customer will often have many “touches” before deciding to make a purchase.
Advanced marketers want to better understand a user’s journey so they can correctly
attribute Conversions and revenue to the correct traffic sources.
When you stop to think about the fact that a user could click a dozen links before
making a purchase, you start to realize how large of a discrepancy this can create. If
you only look at last click attribution, your first click would be deemed a losing
campaign (and vice-versa). The good news is that Analytics provides a blended model…
VIDEO TRAINING
UTM Builder Video Tour
10xresource.com/s17
(provided by 10X)
UTM Documentation
10xresource.com/s19
(provided by Google)
TIP: When sending traffic from Google Ads you can automatically capture
significant information without needing UTM links. This is done by what is
called “auto-tagging”, which is an Analytics setting that is turned on by default.
UNDERSTANDING ATTRIBUTION
Simple Attribution Models:
First Click: Traffic source that originally sent the user gets credit for sale.
Last Click: Traffic source clicked on last before purchase gets credit for sale.
Options in your Analytics property settings
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 37 of 48 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
38
For years, people thought I was crazy when I invested in
building my brand. I knew it would pay off with time. If you are
in for the long haul, you don’t always need to ROI immediately.
By default, Analytics uses what they call their “Cross-channel data driven” model. This
option uses machine learning to try to best attribute the most important touchpoints to
your revenue. Unless you’re spending significant money on direct response ads, you’ll
likely want to stick with this option.
In addition to choosing which attribution model to use, you can also decide on how long
you want the attribution window to be. This is referred to as the “Lookback window”,
which is set at 30 days by default.
The most important thing to realize at this point is that attribution is a critical aspect
in your marketing, both online and offline. While there is no perfect model, having
awareness and looking further into a user’s history will uncover extremely valuable
insights.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

involved in bringing this all to life…

PHASE 1 Plan
While much of this manual has been about planning, here are some questions to pull it
all together…
What target goals should you be driving toward every single day?
What statistics should you be tracking? Which are your KPIs?
What websites or funnels do you need to install Tag Manager on?
What Analytics Conversions do you need to set up on what pages?
What dashboards do you need created? Which are most important?
How are you keeping it all organized? (hint, use Google Sheets!)

While you should immediately start setting big targets, when
it comes to the tech setup, make sure you have a well thought
out strategy. You know your business better than anyone.
It will also help if you think about your statistics initiatives in phases. You won’t be able
to do everything at once, but if you create grouped lists of tasks it will help you get
progress faster…
This is just for illustrative purposes, but the key is being systematic in your approach.
Don’t worry about things being perfect, the most important thing is that you get
started!
List out all stats we want to track
Select which ones are key KPIs
Establish a few main company targets
Set up Tag Manager on all sites
Install Analytics on all websites
Create main KPI dashboard in Sheets
Review targetz every morning at 9:00 am

Hire someone to build our Data Studio
Get all data sources into Data Studio

Start log to track adjustments in meetings

to download the 10X Statistics Checklists! They

When setting up your systems, constantly ask yourself “Do
these numbers make sense?” Don’t assume a so-called expert
will set things up correctly the first time. They rarely do.
During a big event I ask my team to send me stats every
hour. While this obviously isn’t efficient, I don’t just do it for
me. I want my team looking at the numbers constantly.
PHASE 2 Setup
With your plan in place, you should get to immediate work at getting your systems
working. Remember that everything starts with getting good, clean data. Create your
Google accounts and then get Tag Manager and Analytics in place ASAP.

If you have any statistics that require manual collection, you should begin the process of
having that data immediately. The frequency of updates, whether it be daily, weekly or
monthly, will depend on the importance of the information and the staffing you have
available.

Continuously quiz your people about their numbers so they get

PHASE 4 Review
While we’ll talk more about how to use statistics in your day-to-day, here is a simple
formula for maximizing your value from stats. When reviewing reports, think of the 3 T’s…
PHASE 5 Optimize
Clearly there is a lot of work involved in creating statistics and reporting. None of it
matters unless you’re using that data to continually increase your efficiency and improve
your bottom line.
Of all the things you should optimize for, there is none more important than the value of
your leads and customers. As the great Dan Kennedy said…
Advertising and other traffic are not cheap. You’ve got to think of the journey your
customer will go on, and optimize every point possible in that journey. For example, a
simplified customer journey might look something like this…
Each of these steps gives you an opportunity to maximize your ROI (return on
investment). While it will take time, your ultimate goal should be to understand the value
of a lead throughout the journey. Here is a basic example…
Targets - Where are we at on what matters most?
Trends - What direction are my KPIs heading?
Tasks - What specific action items do I need to take?
“Whoever can spend the most for a customer wins.”
Original
Opt-In Product
Upsells
Product
Purchase Email
Follow-Up
Sales
Calls
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 42 of 48 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
43
Lifetime customer value is an impossible stat to track. You
should make money from someone forever if you can. Focus
instead on how quickly you can become profitable.
In the example above, you’ll notice that it took more than 30 days to recoup the cost of a
lead. That is not uncommon in today’s market. When you realize that most competitors
won’t go that far, it becomes easy to see how mastering statistics can give you a massive
advantage.
Clearly there is a lot of work to do. While your strategy will depend on the resources you
have available, here are some thoughts on what this could look like…
Average Cost to Acquire the Lead: $12.46
↓
Value of the Lead in 7 Days: $7.59
↓
Value of the Lead in 30 Days: $11.02
↓
Value of the Lead in 60 Days: $14.46
↓
Value of the Lead in 90 Days: $16.22
HIRING AND OUTSOURCING
Plan
You will likely need to own this. Use the checklists, flowcharts and what
you’ve learned in this manual.
Setup
You will likely oversee it initially, with a technical person helping with
installation. Once set up, it is possible for a marketing team member to
make updates.
Collect
If you are using Sheets for reports, it is ideal to have the appropriate team
member manage collection (for example, ad stats collected by your ad
buyer). You may also consider hiring a virtual assistant.
TIP: Always collect the first report data yourself and document the process
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 43 of 48 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
44
I always recommend outsourcing things like tech setup and data
collection. The one thing you can never outsource is becoming
obsessed with the numbers. You live or die by your stats.
Earlier we discussed hiring someone on Fiverr to help set up your tracking
and analytics. If you don’t have a team member to help with data collection,
you can outsource that too. To hire a team member on an ongoing basis we
recommend using Upwork. 10xresource.com/upwork
Review
Until you’re big enough to hire a data analyst, you own this too. Make sure that
reports are in front of all needed parties as frequently as possible. We will
discuss how to do this in the next chapter.
Optimize
While this is everyone’s job, you are likely the person to ensure that
adjustments are being made accordingly based on what the numbers tell you.
As Grant often says, “What you focus on will expand.” Getting the data is half the battle,
but you’ve got to create regular rituals for utilizing the data as well. Here is an example of
what that might look like…
MANAGING BY STATISTICS
CREATING CONSISTENCY
Targets - Review all day, every day
KPIs - Review daily in morning huddle
All Stats - Review weekly in division meetings
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 44 of 48 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
45
Schedule regular meetings to study stats, even if you are a
company of one. Always know where your numbers are,
which way they are going and what you’re doing about it.
Out of sight, out of mind. Do whatever you’ve got to do to put
your numbers in front of you constantly, whether that be on
monitors, on whiteboards, or printed paper taped to the wall.
It is also helpful to create language that everyone can easily understand and use. For
example, in the 10X office each stat will ultimately fall into one of three categories…
Keep in mind that while most stats should go up, there are some that you want to
reduce. Examples include your cost-per-click for ads, or your cost to acquire a new
customer.
When you walk in Cardone Enterprises headquarters you will see stats on boards like
this. The entire office knows where we are on our company targets and each division
knows if they are “up stat” or “down stat” on their KPIs.
Up Stat - A stat that has gone up in referenced period
Down Stat - A stat that has gone up in referenced period
Nonexistent - A stat that is zero and therefore not reporting
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 45 of 48 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
46
DECISION MAKING
You should ALWAYS reference your stats when decisions need to be made. If you were a
fly on the wall in our office, you would hear this type of dialogue every day…
If you don’t have your own stats yet, use somebody else’s.
For example, if you see a competitor spending millions on
ads, you can have some confidence that the model works.
“I think we should do ABC.”
“What does the data show?”
Team member A says…
Team member B replies…
Business should never be run based on theory.
It is a shift in mindset that starts from the top.
5 QUICK TIPS FOR STATS MANAGEMENT
Don’t make decisions too quickly
Sometimes things start slow. Be sure you’ve collected a sufficient
amount of data before changing course.
1
Don’t forget about ‘nonexistent’ stats
When you’re first getting started it can be easy to forget about the
things that aren’t producing any results.
2
Focus most on stats earlier in journey
For example, in a sales funnel you should put more attention on the
opt-in page than the product upsell.
3
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 46 of 48 --

© 2022 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
47
Make all decisions based on targets
While various stats will go up and down, there is no more important
stat than the target. Always keep it first.
4
Always be inquisitive and ask questions
Look at what is behind the numbers and come from fresh angles.
Grant is always questioning things.
5
When you review stats in meetings, it is a good practice to
keep notes about adjustments you’ve made. You can then
start to correlate which changes have which effects.
The last one is important. While you don’t need to become a data analyst, you do need
to constantly be thinking. Here are some quick examples…
Which step of my funnel are people getting stuck on?
What traffic source is generating the most leads?
How can I increase the overall value of a lead?
Which type of emails are making the most sales?
What type of content is driving the most traffic?
Why are our competitors able to spend more on ads?
What should I do less of? What should I do more of?
What is the most important thing to focus on now?

When you get obsessed with statistics, you will stop adding,
and you will start multiplying. Time to 10X baby!
